sections.paragraph:
(compare אָוָה No. III.) in Kal not used, i.q. תָּאָה No. I. to mark, to delineate.

Piel id. 1Sa 21:14, of David when simulating madness: וַיְתָו עַל־דַּלְתֹות הַשַּׁעַר, “and he made marks (scrawls) upon the doors of the gate,” like petulant boys.

Hiphil הִתְוָה תָו to mark, followed by עַל upon any thing, Eze 9:4.

Derivative, תָּו.

II.

תוה

to repent, to be grieved. (Syr. id.)

Hiphil, causat. to make to grieve, to afflict (as a people, God), Psal. 78:41 [to set marks or limits, gives a good sense in the passage, from תָּוָה I.].

III.

תוה

an unused root, i.q. אָוָה No. I., and Arab. ثوى to abide, to dwell, whence תָּא a chamber; which see, for תָּו.
